Scenario.overview: Communication Systems Complex A highly skilled threat actor group C0026 has meticulously planned a sophisticated cyber attack targeting communication systems. Their objective is to disrupt operations while maintaining stealth and persistence. They employ a multi-stage approach involving extensive reconnaissance, resource development, and multiple phases of exploitation.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Command & Control Server, Network Traffic, Network Share, DNS Record, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Web Shell, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Network Share Access (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers conduct active scanning of network traffic to map out the communication system's topology, identifying potential entry points and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers acquire and setup command and control servers via internet scans, preparing the infrastructure necessary for malware deployment.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromise trusted third-party software updates, gaining initial access to the communication system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: By exploiting known vulnerabilities in public-facing applications, the attackers execute malicious code to establish a foothold within the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain persistent access, the attackers deploy a web shell on the command and control server, enabling remote administration.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers escalate privileges within the system to gain higher-level permissions, facilitating deeper infiltration.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: To evade detection, the attackers obfuscate their malicious payloads, making them less detectable by security tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials using Mimikatz, enabling lateral movement across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: After gaining access, the attackers scan the network to discover valuable targets and map the internal structure.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers leverage legitimate remote services to move laterally throughout the network undetected.

stage.name: Collection
stage.technique_name: T1039: Data from Network Shared Drive
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from network shares, compromising intellectual property and confidential communications.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data exfiltration occurs over alternative protocols like DNS, concealing the transfer of stolen information to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The final phase involves inhibiting system recovery mechanisms, ensuring the compromised systems remain non-operational.

Scenario.summary: The communication system faces a sophisticated and multi-faceted cyber attack orchestrated by the expert threat actor group C0026. With a focus on persistence and impact, the attackers systematically infiltrate, manipulate, and ultimately disable critical systems, demonstrating a high level of skill and determination.
